title: Portfolio Progress Trend — historical per-sprint scope tracking via activity ledger
impact: TotalScopeEffort is now computed historically for each sprint in the selected range instead of using a single current-state snapshot. The handler replays Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.Effort change events from the ActivityEventLedger to reconstruct the total backlog scope as it was at each sprint's end date. Items created after a sprint's end are excluded; items currently in 'Removed' state that were removed after a sprint's end are included. TotalScopeChangePts in the summary now reflects actual scope growth or contraction across the selected range. Accuracy improves as more history accumulates in the ledger.

title: Portfolio Progress Trend — stock-and-flow model with Combined Flow graph
impact: The Portfolio Progress Trend page has been refactored from a % Done / scope snapshot view into a stock-and-flow product-dynamics model. Graph 1 is now a Combined Flow chart showing Completed (green), Added (orange, proxy from PlannedEffort), and Net Flow (blue, dominant) per sprint — Net > 0 means the backlog is shrinking. Graphs 2–4 retain Total Scope (Stock), Remaining Effort (Stock), and Throughput Trend. The summary block now shows Cumulative Net Flow, Total Scope Change, Remaining Effort Change, and classifies the range as Contracting / Stable / Expanding. The page auto-loads with All Products, the first available team, and the current sprint + past 4 sprints (5 total) as the default selection. The % Done graph has been removed. A documented limitation note explains that Added Effort uses PlannedEffort as an inflow proxy and cannot distinguish newly created items from re-estimations.

title: Pipeline Trend — Sprint-based PO view replaces Pipeline Insights
impact: The /home/pipelines page has been redesigned from an engineering telemetry table (single-sprint KPI tiles and stacked charts) into a sprint-bucketed PO trend page. It now shows 4 signals in a compact 2×2 grid: Reliability Trend (success rate %), Time-to-Green Trend (median build duration on main/master branch when data is available; otherwise all-branch fallback), Tail Risk Trend (P90 duration, gaps shown for fewer than 3 runs), and Flakiness/Instability Trend. Each chart includes a slope badge (Improving / Stable / Worsening). A sprint range selector (team, product, end sprint, count) replaces the single-sprint/multi-sprint toggle. A collapsed Advanced/Drill-down panel retains per-sprint detail including P75 and main-branch run counts.

title: Velocity Trend cleanup — signals embedded in Sprint Trend & Planning
impact: The standalone Velocity Trend tile has been removed from Trends. Velocity distribution (median, P25–P75 band) and predictability ratio are now embedded directly in Sprint Trend (Calibration panel, visible in advanced multi-sprint mode) and Planning (Capacity Confidence block). The Trends page is now more focused; calibration signals appear in their tactical and forecasting contexts.
